title: How Much Office Space Per Person Do You Actually Need?
metaTitle: Office Space Per Person Australia: How Much Do You Need? | Your Office Space
metaDescription: The standard office benchmark is 10–15 sqm per person, but hybrid workplaces now work with 7–10 sqm. Here's how to calculate the right size for your team.
excerpt: Most businesses either over-lease or under-lease. Here's a practical guide to calculating the right amount of office space per person for your team in Australia.
author: Joe Kelley
body:
## How much office space do you need per person?

The traditional benchmark for office space in Australia is 10–15 square metres per person. Modern hybrid workplaces — where not everyone is in the office every day — can operate effectively with 7–10 sqm per person. The right number for your business depends on how you work, not just how many people you have.

## Why the benchmark has changed

Fifteen years ago, most offices assumed everyone was in every day. Each person had a dedicated desk, often a private office or at minimum a fixed workstation. The 12–15 sqm rule covered the desk, plus a share of meeting rooms, breakout space, and circulation.

Post-pandemic, most office-based businesses in Australia operate on some form of hybrid model. Not everyone is in every day. That changes the maths — but it doesn't mean you just divide headcount by 0.7 and multiply by 7 sqm. If you get this wrong, you'll be in a space that doesn't work for your team.

## The key variables

### 1. Actual attendance rate

What percentage of your team is in the office on any given day? If you have 30 staff and typically 20 are in at peak, you're planning for 20 people, not 30. But you should add a buffer — 10–15% — for peak days.

### 2. Private offices

Private offices take more space per person than open plan. A private office for one person typically uses 12–18 sqm. If you have multiple directors or managers requiring enclosed offices, your average sqm per person rises.

### 3. Meeting rooms

A business that runs a lot of client meetings needs more meeting room space. Budget roughly 15–25 sqm per meeting room, plus circulation. If you need three meeting rooms for 20 staff, that's 45–75 sqm before you've put a single desk in.

### 4. Work style

Do your people need focused, quiet work most of the day? Or is it a collaborative, high-movement environment? Focused work often requires more space per person (to reduce noise and visual distraction). Collaborative environments can work with less individual space if the shared areas are well designed.

## Office space benchmarks by company type

| Company Type | Typical sqm per person | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Traditional professional services (law, accounting) | 12–18 sqm | Private offices common |
| Corporate open plan | 10–12 sqm | Fixed desks, all staff in daily |
| Modern hybrid office | 7–10 sqm | Activity-based, not everyone in daily |
| Tech / creative / startup | 6–9 sqm | High density, collaborative design |
| Call centre / back office | 5–7 sqm | Minimal individual space, high density |
| Executive / boutique office | 15–25 sqm | Higher per-person allocation |

## A practical example

You have 25 staff. On an average day, 18 are in. You need:

- 18 workstations (assume 4.5 sqm each = 81 sqm)
- 2 medium meeting rooms (20 sqm each = 40 sqm)
- Kitchen and breakout (20 sqm)
- Reception (10 sqm)
- Circulation and storage (allow 20%)

Base area: 151 sqm + 20% = roughly **180 sqm**

At 7.2 sqm per person on your total headcount of 25, that's in the right range for a modern hybrid team.

## Common mistakes

- **Leasing on total headcount rather than peak attendance** — you end up paying for empty desks every day
- **Forgetting meeting rooms in the calculation** — they take up more space than people expect
- **Not leaving a growth buffer** — if you're growing, lease for where you'll be in 2–3 years, not today
- **Underestimating storage** — physical files, equipment, and supplies need somewhere to go

## Use the Office Size Calculator

If you want a quick estimate for your team, use the [Your Office Space Office Size Calculator](/calculators/office-size). Enter your headcount, hybrid attendance rate, and work style, and it'll give you a recommended floor area range.

Or talk to us directly. We help businesses across Australia find the right size space — and avoid paying for square metres they don't need.
